intro.uz: Plato (plateau) — oʻrganishda oʻzimizni toʻxtab qolgandek his qiladigan tekis davr. George Leonardning “Mastery” kitobiga tayanib, AJ platolarni sabr bilan qabul qilishni oʻrgatadi: aslida miya shu paytda ham tinmay oʻrganadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi shu his bilan bogʻliq: uchinchi shart — oʻtmish farazi (third conditional) va wish / if only — orzu va afsus. Allen haqidagi POV hikoyada ikkalasining ildizini eshitasiz.
intro.en: A plateau is a flat stretch in learning where you feel stuck. Drawing on George Leonard's book “Mastery”, AJ teaches you to accept plateaus calmly — your brain is still learning underneath. This week's two grammar topics grow out of that feeling: the third conditional (unreal past) and wish / if only (regret and unreal desire). You'll hear the seeds of both in the POV story about Allen.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻtmishdagi bir tanlov boshqacha boʻlganida nima yuz berishini toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz (“If I had …, I would have …”) va afsus yoki orzuni tabiiy ifodalay olasiz (“I wish I had …; if only I could …”).
canDo.en: By the end you can say out loud how the past would have been different (“If I had …, I would have …”) and express regret or a wish naturally (“I wish I had …; if only I could …”).

grammar.titleUz: Uchinchi shart: oʻtmish farazi (agar … qilganimda edi)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking & Writing — talk about how the past could have been different
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can speculate about unreal past situations (If I had …, I would have …)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha uchinchi shart bitta qolip bilan tugaydi: “…qilganimda edi, …boʻlardi”. Ingliz tilida esa ikkala qism ham oʻtmishga suriladi: shart qismida past perfect (had + V3), natija qismida would have + V3. “agar … qilganimda edi” = If + had + V3; “…boʻlardi” = would have + V3.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ If I would have practised more, I would improve. ✅ If I had practised more, I would have improved. Sababi: if-qismida hech qachon “would” ishlatilmaydi — u faqat natija qismida turadi; oʻtmish farazi uchun had + V3.

examples.en: If Allen had exercised sooner, he wouldn't have suffered for 11 months.
examples.uz: Agar Allen ertaroq mashq qilganida edi, 11 oy azob chekmasdi.

examples.en: If I had listened every day, my English would have improved faster.
examples.uz: Agar har kuni tinglaganimda edi, inglizcham tezroq yaxshilangan boʻlardi.

examples.en: She could have passed the exam if she hadn't quit.
examples.uz: Agar tashlab qoʻymaganida, imtihondan oʻta olardi.

exercises.prompt: If I ___ more last year, I wouldn't have plateaued.
exercises.hintUz: if-qismi → past perfect: had + V3 (had practised)

exercises.prompt: If she had trained sooner, she ___ so long.
If Allen ___ in bed for 11 months, he would have recovered faster.
exercises.hintUz: oʻtmish farazining inkori → had not (hadn't) + V3

exercises.promptUz: Oʻz hayotingizdan rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “If I had … , I would have … .” — oʻtgan bir tanlovingiz haqida.
exercises.hintUz: If + had + V3, keyin would have + V3

grammar.titleUz: wish / if only — orzu va afsus
grammar.titleEn: wish / if only — regret & unreal desire
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — express regret and wishes naturally
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can express regret about the past and unreal wishes (I wish I had …; if only I could …)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha “koshki / qani endi” bilan feʼl orzu shaklida turadi. Ingliz tili esa feʼlni bir pogʻona orqaga suradi: hozirgi orzu → past simple (I wish I had time), oʻtmish afsusi → past perfect (I wish I had studied). “koshki … boʻlsa” = I wish + past; “qani endi … qilganimda edi” = I wish + had + V3.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I wish I studied harder at school. ✅ I wish I had studied harder at school. Oʻtmishdan afsus → wish + past perfect (had + V3). Oddiy past faqat hozirgi orzu uchun ishlatiladi.

examples.en: I wish I had started listening to English sooner.
examples.uz: Qani endi ingliz tilini ertaroq tinglashni boshlaganimda edi.

examples.en: If only I could break through this plateau!
examples.uz: Koshki bu platodan oʻtib keta olsam!

examples.en: Allen wishes he hadn't stayed in bed for so long.
examples.uz: Allen shuncha koʻp yotib qolmaganini istaydi (afsuslanadi).

exercises.prompt: If only I ___ speak fluently now!
Allen wishes he ___ the second doctor first.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda ikkita gap ayting: biri hozirgi orzu (“I wish I could … now”), biri oʻtmish afsusi (“I wish I had … then”).
exercises.hintUz: hozir → wish + past; oʻtmish → wish + had + V3

vocab.defEn: mixed in randomly with a larger group
vocab.example: A few good pictures were scattered among the pile.
The photos showed the thrill of victory.
You could see the agony of defeat on his face.
During the race his face was serene.
The doctor told him to avoid strenuous exercise.

vocab.defEn: calm and in control, especially under stress
vocab.example: His body was strained but his face stayed composed.

vocab.uz: plato; oʻrganishda toʻxtab qolgandek tuyulgan tekis davr
vocab.defEn: a period of little or no progress
vocab.example: Every learner hits a plateau at some point.
With daily practice, progress is inevitable.

vocab.defEn: a sudden, short burst of improvement
vocab.example: Improvement comes in spurts, not steadily.

vocab.defEn: lasting for a long time; not temporary
vocab.example: Mastery is one of the most enduring things in life.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda mijozning kompyuteri “buzilib qoladi” va u vahima qiladi; Todd esa uni tinchlantiradi: “calm down”. Bu AJ mavzusiga mos: plato paytida ham hammasi buzilgandek tuyuladi, lekin vazmin (composed) qolish kerak. Sizningcha, muammo haqiqiy virusmi yoki mijoz shunchaki vahima qilyaptimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a client panics because his computer “broke”, and Todd tells him to calm down. It fits AJ's theme: on a plateau everything feels broken too, but you must stay composed. Do you think it's a real virus, or is the client just panicking?

keyVocab.defEn: to become quiet and relaxed after being upset
a small computer that is easy to carry
an electronic display that shows images
a program designed to harm a computer
a program that finds and removes computer viruses
software that secretly gathers information about you

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.en: Hello, thanks for calling 123TechHelp, I’m Todd. How can I help you?

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: Hello? Can you help me? My computer! Oh man...

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.en: It’s okay sir, calm down. What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: I turned on my laptop and it broke! I mean, the monitor went black!

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.en: Ok, sir, it sounds like you might have a virus.

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: I don’t feel sick,...let me check... Nope! No fever, I’m fine.

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.en: No, your computer might have a virus, I mean, it has a bad program on it. Maybe that’s why it crashed. I recommend that you run an antivirus program in order to safely remove any unwanted spyware or Trojans.

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: Phew! . . . . . . Wait a minute, CRASH??!! Spyware? Trojans! What? where? when?!

quiz.q: In the survey, what percentage of people said they wouldn't go out into the countryside because it wouldn't make good social media photos?
quiz.qUz: Soʻrovda qancha foiz odam qishloqqa chiqmasligiga sabab qilib “ijtimoiy tarmoq uchun yaxshi surat chiqmaydi” deganini aytdi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) taxminan 30%. Catherine 10% deb taxmin qildi, lekin xato qildi — dastur oxirida Neil haqiqiy raqam taxminan 30% ekanini aytadi.

vocab.uz: (noqulay sharoitda) tashqariga chiqmoq, chiqishga jurʼat qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to go out somewhere, usually when conditions are bad
people who became adults in the early 21st century
the generation who became adults in the 1980s and 1990s
amazing, surprising and hard to believe

vocab.uz: chalgʻituvchi narsa, diqqatni boshqa yoqqa tortadigan narsa
vocab.defEn: something that takes your attention away from what matters
suitable or good enough to post on Instagram

funEnglish.title: How to Break Through a Learning Plateau in English

speakingPrompt.en: Think about a time you felt stuck on a plateau — with English, sport, or anything. What would have happened if you had done things differently? What do you wish you had done?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: uchinchi shart (If I had …, I would have …) va wish / if only (I wish I had …; if only I could …).
